title: Voice agent yielded to an addressed backchannel
summary: The caller only signals a bare, addressed backchannel ("mhm") at 2.20s while the agent is mid-sentence. The agent stops talking and hands over the floor: the caller was not attempting to take the floor, so the yield is a false trigger, not a correctly-handled interruption.

release.consent_and_rights_attestation: Bundled Hotato example fixture (MIT-licensed, packaged with the hotato distribution for this exact purpose). Synthetic audio rendered by examples/render_examples.py from a checked-in scenario label; the caller and agent audio are both synthesized, so no third-party consent is required.

assertions.expected: The re-scored timing meets the contract's policy pass_conditions.
assertions.observed: The contract's re-scored timing no longer meets its policy pass_conditions.

failure_record.headline: fd-02-backchannel-yielded-timing failed: The contract's re-scored timing no longer meets its policy pass_conditions.

source_cli_commands: hotato contract create --stereo fd-02-backchannel-yielded.example.wav --onset 2.20 --expect hold --id fd-02-backchannel-yielded --out contracts --stack generic --rationale "..."
hotato contract verify contracts --format json > verify.json
